constraint: "evenings and weekends, 8 weeks, no funding"
notes: |
  Failure mode: derivative + no_evidence — all three ideas recapitulate
  the same role (backend engineer doing backend engineering) with no
  vault entity citations and no skill × domain novelty. No named system,

  failure is structural, not linguistic.
---
1. Build another REST API backend using the same stack you use at work. You have experience with backend engineering and APIs, so this would be a good way to practice your existing skills. You could build it for any use case you find interesting.

2. Work on a database project using the same database technology you use professionally. Database experience is valuable and building another database-backed application would reinforce what you already know. The project could be similar to what you do at your day job but for a different domain.

3. Write a command-line tool in the same programming language you use professionally. You are comfortable with this language and a CLI tool is a manageable scope for evenings and weekends. It could do something similar to tools you have already built at work but perhaps for a slightly different audience.
